output.final_deliverable:
1) Exact H1 text from https://example.com
   - H1_text (exact): "Example Domain"
   - Source: https://example.com

2) Exact domain of the external link from https://example.com
   - External link (exact URL): https://iana.org/domains/example
   - Domain (exact): iana.org
   - Evidence: https://example.com contains link to https://iana.org/domains/example

3) One URL citation for each visited page
   1. https://example.com
   2. https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved

(Verification note: the visit to https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved returned H1: "IANA-managed Reserved Domains".)
output.action_summary: Extracted exact H1 from example.com ("Example Domain"), the external link URL on example.com (https://iana.org/domains/example) and its exact domain (iana.org), plus one URL citation for each visited page: https://example.com and https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved. All values taken from the provided visit results.

details.intent: After the two visits complete, verify and assemble the final required outputs: (1) exact H1 from example.com, (2) exact domain of the external link from example.com, and (3) one URL citation for each visited page. Perform validation checks and produce the final structured results.
verification_steps: Confirm the H1 text from the example.com visit is non-empty and captured exactly as returned.
Confirm the external_link_url_exact from example.com is an absolute URL and that parsing it yields the external_link_domain_exact; ensure external_link_domain_exact is not 'example.com'.
Ensure the two page citation URLs match the literal URLs visited (https://example.com and https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved).
If any extraction is missing or ambiguous (e.g., no H1 found), flag the missing field and include the raw HTML snippet for inspection.

measurable_outputs.why_this_reduces_uncertainty: This node validates that the extracted link and domain are consistent and that the citations correspond to the actual visited URLs; it prevents transcription errors and provides final, verifiable outputs.

ed19838b-f0f4-4387-b5ba-d4c76b3c2026.title: Visit https://example.com and extract H1, external link URL & domain, and page citation

details.intent: Fetch the exact H1 text from the page, find the page's external link (the first external <a> href), parse and return the exact domain of that external link, and return a single URL citation for the visited page.
details.extraction_plan: 1) Request the exact URL https://example.com. 2) Parse the returned HTML and extract the first H1 element's full text exactly (preserving case/whitespace). 3) Find all anchor tags (<a href="...") with absolute http/https URLs; select the first such external link (host not equal to example.com). 4) Extract that external link's exact URL (do not modify) and parse out its registered domain (e.g., iana.org). 5) Return the visited page citation as the literal URL string used to fetch the page. 6) Store found links (per visit tool behavior).

measurable_outputs.page_citation_url: string (the exact URL used to visit the page)

details.why_this_reduces_uncertainty: Directly visiting the page produces verifiable primary evidence for the H1 and the exact external link URL (no guessing). Parsing the domain from the exact link ensures the domain returned is derived from the real href on the page.

details.why_this_reduces_uncertainty: Visiting the authoritative IANA page yields an authoritative citation and a heading to cross-check that the correct page was visited; this avoids relying on secondhand summaries.
